seed_problem: Frontier-Eng JobShop / ALE-Bench: emit a machine schedule for a JSSP instance; checker validates precedence + no-overlap and scores 100*optimum/makespan.
mutation: input+theme: keep the makespan-minimization goal but skin it as mountain-rescue relays and replace the unknown optimum in the score denominator/numerator with a checker-computed, always-feasible serial baseline B = total work, so scoring is deterministic (ratio = 0.1*B/F, capped at 1.0). Fresh randomized square job-shop instances with a difficulty ladder (skewed bottleneck legs at high testId).
title: Mountain Rescue Relays: Minimum-Makespan Relay Schedule
strategies: serial baseline: run every leg one after another on a single global timeline (feasible, makespan = total work, ratio 0.1)
SPT list scheduling: repeatedly dispatch the ready leg with shortest duration at its earliest feasible slot
MWKR / LWKR dispatch rules: prioritize the mission with most (or least) remaining work
multi-restart randomized list scheduling: try many random static mission priorities and keep the shortest-makespan schedule found
local search / shifting-bottleneck style refinement on top of a good dispatch
